message:
feat(extract): add comprehensive HTTP content negotiation system

Implement full content negotiation per RFC 7231 with support for
Accept, Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding, and Accept-Charset headers.

MediaType parsing:
- Parse MIME types like "text/html; charset=utf-8"
- Extract type, subtype, and optional parameters
- Handle quality values (q=0.8) for preference ordering

ContentNegotiator provides:
- Accept header parsing with wildcard matching (*/*, text/*)
- Quality-weighted preference sorting
- Best-match selection from available content types
- Language, encoding, and charset negotiation

Also includes:
- App handler improvements
- Middleware enhancements for content type detection
- Response helpers for content type setting
- Static file serving refinements

Syncs beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-zgme","title":"Implement dependency tree display","description":"## Task: Dependency Tree Display

### Parent Feature
Phase 3: Advanced Output Components (bd-1lkf)

### Overview
Create a visual tree display of the dependency injection graph, showing how dependencies are resolved and detecting circular dependencies.

### Background & Reasoning
Dependency injection can become complex in larger applications. Visualizing the dependency graph helps developers:
- Understand what depends on what
- Identify circular dependency issues before runtime
- Optimize resolution order
- Debug 'missing dependency' errors by seeing the full chain

### Technical Implementation

#### Data Structures
```rust
// crates/fastapi-output/src/components/dependency_tree.rs

pub struct DependencyNode {
    pub name: String,
    pub type_name: String,
    pub scope: DependencyScope,
    pub children: Vec<DependencyNode>,
    pub is_circular_ref: bool,
}

pub enum DependencyScope {
    Singleton,   // Application lifetime
    Request,     // Per-request
    Transient,   // New instance each time
}

pub struct DependencyTreeDisplay {
    roots: Vec<DependencyNode>,
    show_types: bool,
    show_scopes: bool,
    highlight_cycles: bool,
}
```

#### Visualization

**Rich Mode (Tree with Colors)**:
```
┌────────────── Dependency Graph ──────────────┐
│                                               │
│ DatabasePool (singleton)                      │
│ └── type: sqlx::Pool<Postgres>               │
│                                               │
│ UserService (request)                         │
│ ├── DatabasePool                              │
│ └── CacheService                              │
│     └── RedisPool (singleton)                 │
│                                               │
│ AuthService (request)                         │
│ ├── UserService                               │
│ └── TokenValidator (singleton)                │
│                                               │
│ ⚠ CYCLE DETECTED:                            │
│   ServiceA → ServiceB → ServiceA              │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Plain Mode**:
```
Dependency Graph:
  DatabasePool (singleton)
  UserService (request) <- DatabasePool, CacheService
  CacheService (request) <- RedisPool
  AuthService (request) <- UserService, TokenValidator
  WARNING: Circular dependency: ServiceA -> ServiceB -> ServiceA
```

#### Implementation
```rust
impl DependencyTreeDisplay {
    pub fn display(&self) {
        let output = RichOutput::global();
        
        if !output.should_render() {
            self.display_plain(&output);
            return;
        }
        
        let theme = output.theme();
        let mut tree_nodes = Vec::new();
        
        for root in &self.roots {
            tree_nodes.push(self.build_tree_node(root, theme, 0));
        }
        
        // Check for cycles
        let cycles = self.detect_cycles();
        
        let mut content_parts: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
        
        for node in tree_nodes {
            let tree = Tree::new(node);
            content_parts.push(format!("{}", tree.render_to_string()));
        }
        
        if !cycles.is_empty() {
            content_parts.push(String::new());
            content_parts.push(theme.style_text("⚠ CYCLES DETECTED:", "red bold"));
            for cycle in cycles {
                content_parts.push(format!("  {}", cycle.join(" → ")));
            }
        }
        
        let panel = Panel::from_text(&content_parts.join("\n"))
            .title("Dependency Graph")
            .rounded();
        
        output.renderable(&panel);
    }
    
    fn build_tree_node(&self, dep: &DependencyNode, theme: &FastApiTheme, depth: usize) -> TreeNode {
        let scope_indicator = match dep.scope {
            DependencyScope::Singleton => theme.style_text("(singleton)", "blue"),
            DependencyScope::Request => theme.style_text("(request)", "green"),
            DependencyScope::Transient => theme.style_text("(transient)", "yellow"),
        };
        
        let label = if dep.is_circular_ref {
            theme.style_text(&format!("{} {} ↻", dep.name, scope_indicator), "red")
        } else {
            format!("{} {}", dep.name, scope_indicator)
        };
        
        let mut node = TreeNode::new(label);
        
        if self.show_types {
            node.add_child(TreeNode::new(
                theme.style_text(&format!("type: {}", dep.type_name), "dim")
            ));
        }
        
        for child in &dep.children {
            node.add_child(self.build_tree_node(child, theme, depth + 1));
        }
        
        node
    }
    
    fn detect_cycles(&self) -> Vec<Vec<String>> {
        // DFS-based cycle detection
        let mut cycles = Vec::new();
        let mut visited = HashSet::new();
        let mut path = Vec::new();
        
        for root in &self.roots {
            self.find_cycles(root, &mut visited, &mut path, &mut cycles);
        }
        
        cycles
    }
}
```

### Integration with DI System
```rust
// In fastapi-core/src/depends.rs

impl DependencyContainer {
    pub fn display_graph(&self) {
        let roots = self.build_dependency_nodes();
        
        DependencyTreeDisplay {
            roots,
            show_types: true,
            show_scopes: true,
            highlight_cycles: true,
        }.display();
    }
}
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] DependencyNode and DependencyTreeDisplay structs
- [ ] Recursive tree building with depth tracking
- [ ] Scope coloring (singleton=blue, request=green, transient=yellow)
- [ ] Circular dependency detection with DFS
- [ ] Cycle highlighting in red with ↻ indicator
- [ ] Type information display (optional)
- [ ] Plain text fallback
- [ ] Integration with DependencyContainer
- [ ] Unit tests for cycle detection

### Dependencies
- bd-36uw (RichOutput facade)
- bd-14v4 (FastApiTheme)
- Access to fastapi-core DependencyContainer

### Considerations
- Deep dependency trees should be truncated with '...' indicator
- Consider caching the graph visualization for large apps
- Cycle detection must handle self-referential deps","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:12:48.877004850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:31.691597921Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:28:31.691509544Z","close_reason":"Implemented - dependency_tree.rs complete with DependencyNode, DependencyTreeDisplay, cycle detection, and tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-14v4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-1lkf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-zgme","depends_on_id":"bd-36uw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-0ar","title":"Connection acceptance with limits","description":"Implement connection limiting in server:
- Max concurrent connections limit
- Per-IP connection limits (optional)
- Connection queue with backpressure
- Graceful rejection with 503 when at limit

DEPENDS ON: TCP server with asupersync

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Server respects max_connections setting
- Excess connections rejected gracefully
- No resource exhaustion under load
- Metrics: current connection count

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Limit enforcement
- Integration: Behavior at limit
- Load: Many simultaneous connections
- Recovery: After connections close","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:39:42.844827923Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:21.648034840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T11:17:21.647979135Z","close_reason":"Implemented connection acceptance with limits:
- Added connection_counter AtomicU64 to TcpServer for tracking active connections
- Implemented try_acquire_connection() with compare-exchange for thread-safe limit checking
- Implemented release_connection() for decrementing counter
- Added current_connections() public method for metrics
- Connection limit enforced in accept_loop before handling
- Returns 503 Service Unavailable with Connection: close when limit reached
- Added 8 unit tests for connection limiting behavior","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["limits","phase-1","server"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0ar","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-9ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"fastapi_rust-0mi","title":"Content negotiation via Accept headers","description":"Support server-driven content negotiation via Accept, Accept-Encoding, Accept-Language headers. Allow handlers to return different representations based on client preferences.

**Implementation:**
- Parse Accept header with quality values (q=)
- Parse Accept-Encoding for compression negotiation
- Parse Accept-Language for localization
- Content-Type selection based on Accept
- Vary header generation
- 406 Not Acceptable when no match

**Components:**
- AcceptHeader extractor with parsed preferences
- negotiate_content_type() helper
- Vary header auto-generation
- Integration with response serialization

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Accept: application/json,text/html;q=0.9 prefers JSON
- Accept-Encoding respected for compression
- Vary header includes Accept when used
- 406 when requested type not available
- Wildcard matching works

**Tests:**
- Handler returns JSON for Accept: application/json
- Handler returns HTML for Accept: text/html
- Quality values respected in ordering
- 406 for unsupported Accept type
- Vary header included in response","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:53.745765535Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:53.745765535Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","negotiation","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-0mi","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-qii","title":"Security test suite","description":"Security-focused test suite:
- HTTP smuggling payloads
- Header injection attempts
- Path traversal attempts
- Oversized request handling
- Malformed UTF-8 handling
- Known CVE patterns

TEST CATEGORIES:
1. Request smuggling (CL.TE, TE.CL)
2. Header injection (CRLF)
3. Path traversal (../)
4. Resource exhaustion
5. Encoding attacks

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All known attack vectors tested
- Clear pass/fail for each
- Runs in CI","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:45:27.787417791Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T09:20:32.325972806Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T09:20:32.325926108Z","close_reason":"Security test suite complete with 47 tests covering smuggling, injection, traversal, exhaustion, encoding attacks, and CVE patterns","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-qii","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-ikb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"fastapi_rust-qvb","title":"HTTP TRACE method rejection for security","description":"Explicitly disable HTTP TRACE method to prevent XST (Cross-Site Tracing) attacks. TRACE echoes the request back, which can be used to steal credentials.

**Implementation:**
- Reject TRACE requests with 405 Method Not Allowed
- Log TRACE attempts as potential attack
- No configuration to enable TRACE (security)
- Apply before routing layer

**Security Rationale:**
- TRACE can expose Authorization headers via XSS
- No legitimate use case in modern APIs
- OWASP recommends disabling

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- TRACE requests return 405
- TRACE attempts logged as security event
- All routes reject TRACE
- Cannot be enabled per-route

**Tests:**
- TRACE / returns 405
- TRACE /api/users returns 405
- Security log entry for TRACE attempt","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:55.706202630Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:30:04.385438229Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","methods","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-qvb","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-8y3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-rbm","title":"Startup and shutdown hooks","description":"Implement lifecycle hooks:
- on_startup hooks (run before accepting connections)
- on_shutdown hooks (run after drain complete)
- Async hook support
- Hook failure handling
- Ordering of multiple hooks

DEPENDS ON: Graceful shutdown

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Startup hooks run before serve
- Shutdown hooks run after drain
- Hook failure = startup/shutdown failure
- Hooks can be async

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Hook ordering
- Unit: Failure handling
- Integration: Full lifecycle","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T14:43:52.109030002Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T02:32:19.389937347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T17:39:32.880912798Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","lifecycle","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-rbm","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-n12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-spm","title":"Response model validation and serialization","description":"Implement response_model parameter for output validation:

ROUTE PARAMETER:
#[get('/users/{id}', response_model = User)]
async fn get_user(id: Path<i64>) -> Json<User>

BEHAVIOR:
1. Handler returns data
2. Validate against response_model
3. Serialize with configured options
4. Return JSON response

SERIALIZATION OPTIONS (per FastAPI):
- response_model_include: Set<String>
- response_model_exclude: Set<String>
- response_model_by_alias: bool
- response_model_exclude_unset: bool
- response_model_exclude_defaults: bool
- response_model_exclude_none: bool

VALIDATION BEHAVIOR:
- If response doesn't match model: 500 Internal Error
- ResponseValidationError (internal, not exposed to client)
- Log the actual error for debugging

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Response validated before sending
- Serialization options work
- 500 on validation failure (not 422)
- Error logged for debugging

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each serialization option
- Unit: Validation failure handling
- Unit: Include/exclude fields
- Integration: Full response flow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticBridge","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:13:22.416124841Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:52:51.268055067Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:52:51.267992400Z","close_reason":"Implemented Response model validation and serialization: Added ResponseModelConfig with include/exclude field filtering, exclude_none, exclude_unset, exclude_defaults, and by_alias options. Added ValidatedResponse<T> wrapper that validates and filters responses. Added ResponseModel trait (blanket impl for all Serialize types). Added exclude_fields() and include_fields() helper functions. ValidatedResponse returns 500 with ResponseValidationError on serialization failures. 19 comprehensive tests pass covering all config options and filtering scenarios.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-2","response","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-spm","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-ams","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"fastapi_rust-syy","title":"HTTPS redirect and enforcement middleware","description":"Force HTTPS connections by redirecting HTTP requests and setting HSTS headers. Critical for production security.

**Implementation:**
- Redirect HTTP to HTTPS (301 or 307)
- HSTS header (Strict-Transport-Security)
- Configurable redirect behavior
- Proxy-aware (X-Forwarded-Proto)
- Exclude health check endpoints

**Configuration:**
- redirect: bool (enable/disable redirects)
- hsts_seconds: int (max-age value)
- include_subdomains: bool
- preload: bool (HSTS preload)
- exclude_paths: Vec<String>

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- HTTP requests redirected to HTTPS
- HSTS header set on HTTPS responses
- Health checks accessible over HTTP
- Proxy headers respected
- Configurable per-environment

**Tests:**
- HTTP GET redirects to HTTPS
- HTTPS GET returns HSTS header
- Health check accessible over HTTP
- X-Forwarded-Proto: https not redirected
- HSTS preload flag works","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:58.898720850Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:58.898720850Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["https","middleware","phase-2","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-syy","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-rhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-t3v","title":"OPTIONS method auto-handling for CORS preflight","description":"Automatically handle OPTIONS requests for CORS preflight. When CORS middleware is enabled, generate correct preflight responses with Access-Control-Allow-Methods, Access-Control-Allow-Headers. For non-CORS OPTIONS, return 204 with Allow header listing supported methods for the route.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- OPTIONS requests handled before route handlers
- Allow header populated with route's methods
- CORS preflight generates correct headers
- OPTIONS disabled on routes where not wanted
- No body in OPTIONS response

**Tests:**
- OPTIONS returns Allow header with GET,POST for route supporting both
- CORS preflight returns Access-Control-Allow-Methods
- OPTIONS on non-existent route returns 404
- OPTIONS on method-restricted route returns only allowed methods","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:33.992024336Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:28:09.102856551Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:28:09.102718754Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cors","http","methods","routing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-t3v","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-4ck","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-t3v","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-bc8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"fastapi_rust-t4g","title":"Transfer-Encoding handling and hop-by-hop headers","description":"Properly handle Transfer-Encoding (chunked, gzip, etc.) and strip hop-by-hop headers per HTTP spec. Critical for correct proxying and body handling.

**Implementation:**
- Parse Transfer-Encoding header (comma-separated)
- Handle chunked encoding in body parser
- Strip hop-by-hop headers: Connection, Keep-Alive, Proxy-Authenticate, Proxy-Authorization, TE, Trailers, Transfer-Encoding, Upgrade
- Preserve only end-to-end headers in forwarded requests
- Content-Length vs Transfer-Encoding mutual exclusion

**Hop-by-Hop Headers to Strip:**
- Connection
- Keep-Alive
- Proxy-Authenticate
- Proxy-Authorization
- TE
- Trailers
- Transfer-Encoding
- Upgrade

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Chunked requests decoded correctly
- Hop-by-hop headers stripped when proxying
- Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding mutual exclusion enforced
- Trailers supported with chunked encoding

**Tests:**
- Chunked body decoded correctly
- Response with hop-by-hop headers stripped for proxy
- 400 if both Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding present
- Trailers accessible after chunked body","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:13:09.445341484Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:42:11.498818810Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:42:11.498752457Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented in crates/fastapi-http/src/body.rs: ChunkedReader for sync parsing, AsyncChunkedStream for async streaming. STANDARD_HOP_BY_HOP_HEADERS constant in connection.rs, strip_hop_by_hop_headers() function. Chunked encoding parsing with trailers support and extension handling.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","parser","phase-1"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-t4g","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:33Z","created_by":"import"}]}
